title: rudgea jasminoides subsp. nervosa
intro: Rudgea jasminoides subsp. nervosa, also known as the Nervosa or Brazilian Jasmine, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Rudgea jasminoides subsp. nervosa include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also employed in traditional folk medicine in Brazil and other parts of South America.
